item.title: Customization of the Theme with Configuration
item.author: admin@example.org (Author)
item.description: This page outlines common TailRoad configuration parameters, which can be set either in your Hugo configuration file or directly in a page’s Front Matter.

item.title: Linux Microphone Debugging: From ALSA to PipeWire
item.author: admin@example.org (Author)
item.description: While testing my laptop’s microphone on Linux, I heard sharp static noise instead of my voice but the microphone wasn’t broken. The problem was a format mismatch between raw audio streams. This guide walks through proper Linux microphone debugging: why file recording works when live pipes don’t, how PipeWire differs from ALSA, and the tools (pw-top, pavucontrol) that reveal what’s really happening in your audio stack.

item.title: Contact
item.author: admin@example.org (Author)
item.description:
“I don’t do small talk. But I do deep technical conversation.”

If you’re here, you probably have something worth saying. I’m reachable through the channels below — pick whichever fits your context.

When to Reach Out

I’m open to:

  • Vulnerability research collaborations
  • Kernel security discussions and code review
  • Offensive security consulting engagements
  • CTF team invitations and writeup exchanges
  • Speaking at security conferences or community events
  • Feedback on my published research or tools

I’m not the right contact for:

item.title: Open Source
item.author: admin@example.org (Author)
item.description:
“The best way to learn how things break is to build the tools that break them.”

I believe in open research. Everything here is released for educational purposes — for the security community, kernel engineers, and anyone curious enough to read the source.

Tools & Projects

🔧 [project-name] — Kernel Syscall Tracer

Language: C · Platform: Linux x86_64 · Status: Active

A low-level syscall interception framework built on top of eBPF and raw tracepoints. Designed for tracing kernel behaviour during exploit development without triggering common detection mechanisms.

item.title: About Tailroad Theme
item.author: admin@example.org (Author)
item.description:
About This Hugo Theme

This is a demo of how an “About” page could look. Customize it with:
